recent_news.body_text:
TREVOR SUTTON is a Nonresident Senior Fellow at the Center for International Policy and a Senior Researcher at the Center for Global Energy Policy at Columbia University.
Trump’s Tariff Mess Offers a Chance to Restore Legislative Oversight
Trevor Sutton

recent_news.title: This AI Was Trained Only on Pre-1930 Text. We Asked It About Hitler, Stocks, and the Future
recent_news.summary: Talkie-1930 is a 13-billion-parameter model that has never heard of the internet, World War II, or modern politics. The results are fascinating, funny, and occasionally unsettling.

general_news.title: 用過時資料訓練語言模型的「AI 時光機」，能否準確預測現在？

general_news.title: Vintage chatbot lives in the past like an elderly relative
general_news.summary: Talkie's training data stops at the end of 1930, and its creators hope it'll help us better understand how AI thinks If you're tired of interacting with a bot that spews Nazi propaganda or refers to itself as MechaHitler, you could sign off of Elon Musk's xAI. Or, just to be sure, use an LLM whose training data ends in 1930, three years before the Nazis took power in Germany and nine years before World War II started.…

general_news.title: talkie: an LM from 1930
general_news.summary: Here is the link, with explanation. The post talkie: an LM from 1930 appeared first on Marginal REVOLUTION.

general_news.title: Introducing talkie: a 13B vintage language model from 1930
general_news.summary: Introducing talkie: a 13B vintage language model from 1930 New project from Nick Levine, David Duvenaud, and Alec Radford (of GPT, GPT-2, Whisper fame). talkie-1930-13b-base (53.1 GB) is a "13B language model trained on 260B tokens of historical pre-1931 English text". talkie-1930-13b-it (26.6 GB) is a checkpoint "finetuned using a novel dataset of instruction-response pairs extracted from pre-1931 reference works", designed to power a chat interface.
